reasoning: The 10-year TIPS yield (DFII10) closed at 1.73% on 2025-10-24, which serves as our base value. Over the past few weeks, this value has fluctuated between 1.68% and 1.84%, demonstrating significant daily volatility but no aggressive directional breakout in the last week. The general macroeconomic backdrop in 2025 has seen yields drifting lower (from 2.34% in January to 1.73% in October) as inflation cools and the Fed continues a likely rate-cutting cycle. Because financial time series such as bond yields roughly follow a martingale process, the probability of an increase over a given horizon is approximately 50%, less half the probability of an exact tie. Ties are somewhat likely over a 1-week horizon since the data is rounded to 2 decimal places, reducing P(increase) below 50%. Over longer horizons (1 month and 3 months), the likelihood of an exact tie diminishes, pulling the probability closer to 50%. An additional slight negative tilt is maintained due to the prevailing medium-term downtrend. Thus, the estimates are 47%, 48%, and 49% for the respective forward dates, perfectly reflecting increasing uncertainty (approaching 0.5) over longer horizons.
